title: corydalis adunca subsp. scaphopetala
intro: Corydalis adunca subsp. scaphopetala, also known as the golden corydalis or western corydalis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties, which are attributed to the presence of isoquinoline alkaloids such as protopine and berberine. The medicinal properties of Corydalis adunca subsp. scaphopetala have been used to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, offering potential health benefits for individuals experiencing anxiety, insomnia, and other conditions. In herbal preparations, Corydalis adunca subsp. scaphopetala is often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and capsules, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications and side effects.
